focus: Should mutation_boundaries become a cross-family workflow-YAML convention? RESOLVED: No. Correctly doctor-specific. Schema models filesystem-path-target validation unique to doctor operating model. Other families use different safety models (behavioral/dynamic/git-delegated). Cross-family promotion = over-abstraction. Real priority is within-doctor standardization (fable-mode still missing block).

label: mutation_boundaries correctly doctor-specific; should NOT become cross-family convention

detail: mutation_boundaries present in 10/10 doctor asset files (all except fable-mode which is P1-D2); 0/52 non-doctor YAMLs. Schema models filesystem-path-target validation with allowed_targets/forbidden_targets/validator sub-block.
deep_research_confirm.yaml:47-51 uses forbidden_operations (behavioral dry-run constraints) — semantically different from mutation_boundaries path-target model. Not a promotion candidate.
doctor_code-graph.yaml:93 and doctor_skill-advisor.yaml:99 use approval_gates (human checkpoints) in ADDITION to mutation_boundaries (path-safety). Orthogonal schemas within doctor, not competing.
doctor_fable-mode.yaml still lacks mutation_boundaries block (P1-D2 confirmed open). Uses only prose read_only_invariant at line 20-24.

reason: Schema models filesystem-path-target validation unique to doctor operating model. Other families have different mutation-safety needs: deep uses behavioral forbidden_operations, create/speckit use dynamic paths governed by SCOPE LOCK, sk-git delegates to git. Promoting would be over-abstraction (AGENTS.md wrong-abstraction anti-pattern).
